behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: LLM output quality is the failure boundary: a mocked generator can pass while a real model still returns generic text. Correctness depends on semantic grounding to thread details and can vary across runs.
behaviors.test_description: Run with a real model on a real-looking thread containing 3 concrete points (dates, decisions, open question). Assert the draft explicitly references at least 2 of those points and is not a generic template response.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Main risk is wiring between draft-claim extraction, KB lookup, and contradiction rules. Pure unit mocks can miss schema/lookup/parsing mismatches. Real network/OS is not required if KB is local.
behaviors.test_description: Use real checker modules with a local KB fixture and a draft containing one contradictory claim and one supported claim. Verify contradictory claim is flagged with KB citation and supported claim is not flagged.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Failure is often hallucination behavior from the real model under missing context. Mocked generation can hide this by always returning a compliant disclaimer.
behaviors.test_description: Execute retrieval with an empty/irrelevant KB and call the real drafting model. Assert the output includes the required uncertainty disclaimer and does not introduce unverifiable specific facts (e.g., fabricated numbers/dates not present in thread).

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Critical boundary is orchestration/state gating between draft status, approval event, and send adapter invocation. Unit tests can miss cross-module state transitions; external infra is not inherently required.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator + persistence + send adapter in-process. After draft generation and fact-check, verify send adapter has zero calls before approval; then emit explicit approval and verify exactly one send call.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: This validates the full agent pipeline across multiple stages, including at least one real agentic generation step plus downstream checks and approval gating. Lower-level tests miss cross-step regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real email thread source, run the full pipeline with real draft generation and fact-checking, and verify stage outputs/transitions in order: thread parsed -> draft generated -> fact check results attached -> approval required before send.
